title: multidevice: admin election uses per-user sequence
description:
Fix the MLS group admin election in relay/src/ws.rs and clients so that admin status is per-user (pub_id), not per-device-connection, when a user has multiple devices.

Why: admin = pub_id with lowest connection_seq. With multi-device, a single user has multiple connection seqs (one per device). If device 2 of user Alice connects after user Bob, device 2 gets a higher seq than Bob — but Alice's device 1 (lower seq) is still connected. Alice should still be admin. If the min seq across Alice's devices is lower than Bob's seq, Alice is admin.

What to do:
- connection_seq(pub_id) in state.rs: return min seq across all Vec entries for pub_id (already noted in nie-uyhc but called out explicitly here for the admin logic)
- Audit all call sites of connection_seq and is_admin checks in ws.rs to confirm they use pub_id-level seq, not device-level
- Test: Alice connects device 1 (seq 0), Bob connects (seq 1), Alice connects device 2 (seq 2). Alice's connection_seq = min(0,2) = 0. Alice is still admin. Alice device 1 disconnects. Alice's connection_seq = 2. Bob (seq 1) is now admin.
- Ensure admin promotion on disconnect triggers the correct pub_id (the user whose last device left, not just the connection that closed)

Acceptance: admin election produces the correct result in all single-device scenarios (no regression) and the multi-device scenario described above.
close_reason: connection_seq already returns per-user min (from nie-uyhc); ws.rs uses it correctly; admin election test added to state.rs
